# SL-PERF-006: external-observation-sampled

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-rule-docs.mjs. Do not edit by hand. -->

## Summary

External performance or analytics data is sampled and must be interpreted with
caution.

## Metadata

- Category: Performance and technical signals (performance-technical)
- Default severity: info
- Confidence: certain
- Scope: site
- Provider scope: external
- Version: 1.0.0

## Evidence Sources

- google
- yandex

## Required Evidence

- provider
- sampling-state
- observed-at
- fetched-at

## When This Triggers

Inspect provider observation metadata for sampling status.

## False Positives

False positives are expected to be rare because this rule relies on
deterministic local evidence. Suppress only with a reason when the route
contract intentionally differs from the observed signal.

## How To Fix

Display sampling state and avoid treating sampled data as exact.

## Examples

Bad-case fixture reference:

- fixtures/performance/external-observation-sampled

Fixed direction:

- Display sampling state and avoid treating sampled data as exact.

The generated documentation does not invent HTML, DSL, source locations,
selectors, or external observations beyond the catalog fixture references above.

## Implementation Surfaces

- Google integration must provide dated, freshness-aware observations before
  this rule can use Google evidence.
- Yandex integration must provide dated, freshness-aware observations before
  this rule can use Yandex evidence.
